name: Nicolas Poussin
bio: French painter and the leading figure of 17th-century classical French painting. Known for rational compositions and history paintings rooted in ancient subjects.

name: Peter Paul Rubens
bio: Flemish artist renowned for color-driven dramatic compositions with dynamic movement and sensuous forms. A key proponent of the Baroque's theatrical style.

name: Jean-Antoine Watteau
bio: French painter whose brief career helped define Rococo art. Best known for fêtes galantes — scenes of idyllic aristocratic amusements set in lush landscapes.

name: Jacques-Louis David
bio: Leading French Neoclassical painter who used ancient Roman motifs to convey modern political ideals. Painter to both King Louis XVI and Napoleon.

name: J.-A.-D. Ingres
bio: French Neoclassical painter known for sinuous contour, flattened depth, and idealized form. His work bridges classicism with Romantic sensuality.

name: Benjamin West
bio: Anglo-American painter who innovated the history painting genre by depicting modern events in classical style, notably The Death of General Wolfe.

name: Anton Raphael Mengs
bio: German painter active in Rome, Madrid, and Saxony, who became one of the precursors to Neoclassicism. He was a close friend of Winckelmann and sought to revive classical antiquity.

name: Anne-Louis Girodet
bio: Pupil of David who blended Neoclassical technique with Romantic mood. His Sleep of Endymion exemplifies the transition between the two movements.

name: Francisco Goya
bio: Spanish painter and printmaker who documented war, social corruption, and human irrationality. His work moves from elegant court portraits to dark, haunting visions.

name: Théodore Géricault
bio: French Romantic painter whose Raft of the Medusa shocked audiences with its raw depiction of human suffering and political scandal.

name: Eugène Delacroix
bio: Leading French Romantic painter known for expressive color, dynamic brushwork, and politically charged subjects. Liberty Leading the People is among his most iconic works.

name: J. M. W. Turner
bio: English Romantic landscape painter celebrated for luminous, atmospheric studies of light, weather, and speed. His late works anticipate Impressionism.

name: Caspar David Friedrich
bio: German Romantic painter known for contemplative, allegorical landscapes evoking the sublime. Figures are often seen from behind, gazing into vast nature.

name: Gustave Courbet
bio: French Realist who insisted that art should depict only things the artist can see. His monumental paintings of peasants and laborers challenged academic conventions.

name: Honoré Daumier
bio: French caricaturist, painter, and sculptor whose incisive satirical lithographs chronicled political corruption and social inequality in 19th-century France.

name: Édouard Manet
bio: French painter who bridged Realism and Impressionism. His bold confrontation of the viewer and flattened pictorial space challenged traditional painting conventions.

name: Claude Monet
bio: Founder of French Impressionism whose serial paintings of haystacks, cathedrals, and water lilies capture the fleeting effects of light and atmosphere.

name: Georges Seurat
bio: French painter who pioneered Pointillism, applying systematic dots of pure color on canvas for the eye to optically blend. His methods gave Impressionism a scientific foundation.

name: Paul Signac
bio: French painter who expanded Seurat's Pointillist technique with bolder, mosaic-like color blocks. An influential theorist and champion of Neo-Impressionism.

name: Paul Cézanne
bio: French Post-Impressionist painter who explored the underlying geometry of nature. Often called the father of modern art for his influence on Cubism and abstraction.

name: Vincent van Gogh
bio: Dutch Post-Impressionist master of expressive color, bold brushwork, and emotional intensity. Despite selling few works in his lifetime, he became one of the most influential artists in history.

name: Paul Gauguin
bio: French painter who rejected European conventions and sought spiritual meaning in non-Western cultures. His symbolic, anti-naturalistic style influenced many modern art movements.

name: Edvard Munch
bio: Norwegian painter and printmaker whose deeply personal themes of anxiety, death, love, and alienation helped define Expressionism. The Scream is among the most recognizable images in art history.

name: Henri Matisse
bio: French artist who led the Fauves in liberating color from descriptive function. Throughout his career, he pursued a radical simplification of form while maintaining extraordinary decorative power.

name: Egon Schiele
bio: Austrian Expressionist known for raw, distorted figure drawings and intense self-portraits that explore sexuality, mortality, and psychological vulnerability.

name: Ernst Ludwig Kirchner
bio: German Expressionist painter and co-founder of Die Brücke. His jagged forms and acid colors depict the frenetic alienation of modern city life.

name: Emil Nolde
bio: German-Danish Expressionist famous for vibrant watercolors and thick, vivid brushwork. His paintings of masks and religious ecstasy convey primal emotional force.

name: Wassily Kandinsky
bio: Russian painter and art theorist who pioneered abstract art. He believed that colors and forms could express inner spiritual states without representing the visible world.

name: Pablo Picasso
bio: Spanish painter, sculptor, and printmaker who co-founded Cubism and became the most dominant artist of the 20th century. His relentless experimentation produced an enormous and varied body of work.

name: Constantin Brancusi
bio: Romanian sculptor who reduced forms to their essential shapes, creating elegantly simplified sculptures that bridge Cubism and pure abstraction.

name: Giacomo Balla
bio: Italian Futurist painter who decomposed motion into sequential phases, influenced by chronophotography. His work embodies the Futurist obsession with speed and dynamism.

name: Umberto Boccioni
bio: Italian painter and sculptor who was the principal theorist of Futurism. His dynamic compositions and landmark sculpture Unique Forms of Continuity in Space define the movement's visual language.

name: Luigi Russolo
bio: Italian Futurist painter and composer who created the Art of Noises manifesto, pioneering noise music and expanding artistic expression beyond the visual into the sonic realm.

name: Marcel Duchamp
bio: French-American artist who challenged the very definition of art through readymades and conceptual provocations. His Fountain (1917) remains one of the most consequential gestures in modern art.

name: Gustave Moreau
bio: French Symbolist painter known for his mystical and highly decorative works depicting mythological and religious subjects. His art is characterized by a dreamlike, ornate style.

name: Odilon Redon
bio: French Symbolist painter, printmaker, and draftsman. His early 'noirs'—charcoal drawings and lithographs—are famous for their bizarre, surreal, and dreamlike imagery.

name: Gustav Klimt
bio: Austrian Symbolist painter and a prominent member of the Vienna Secession movement. He is noted for his highly decorative, often eroticized paintings and murals.
